title: Control-plane cache stampede cascaded into stale configuration and API failures
summary: Flag evaluations and config fetches failed across multiple product surfaces after a control-plane incident. The outage looked like a database problem from some dashboards and a CDN glitch from others, but the actual chain requires reasoning from cache invalidation behavior, control-plane rollout timing, database fanout, and repeated client retries across several services.
primary_hypotheses: feature flag cache stampede with downstream overload
operational_context: A product launch at 13:00Z caused a broad increase in feature flag lookups for anonymous traffic.
The control-plane deployment at 12:56Z changed cache invalidation granularity for namespace-level updates.
